title: 'Do you realize that your statements are completely absurd?': Inside the arrest of the Louvre burglars
subhead: In their new book, journalists Jean-Michel Décugis, Nicolas-Charles Torrent and Le Monde's Jérémie Pham-Lê recount the events of the burglary at the Paris museum on October 19, 2025, and the arrest of the suspects. Here are excerpts from the book.

France faces unprecedented early ozone pollution amid heat wave
subhead: Unusually high temperatures in recent days have led to a deterioration in air quality across several regions. NGOs are calling for emergency measures to address this spike in ozone pollution.

title: Gabriel Zucman and Emmanuel Saez, the two 'Frenchies' campaigning to tax California's billionaires
subhead: California's 250 billionaires have amassed staggering wealth while paying very little in taxes, French researchers Gabriel Zucman and Emmanuel Saez denounced in an op-ed for the New York Times. They support a proposal for a one-time 5% wealth tax, which will be put to a referendum on November 3.

title: Second seed Rybakina dumped out of French Open by Starodubtseva
subhead: It is Australian Open champion Elena Rybakina's earliest exit from any WTA tournament since the 2025 Miami Open.

title: At Versailles, Louis XVI's private chamber is recreated after 40 years of twists and turns
subhead: Reconstructed from a single fabric sample and written archives, the recreation of the room and bed where the king slept alone went through its share of extraordinary episodes before finally coming to fruition.

title: Trump seeks deal to neutralize Iran's highly enriched uranium stockpile
subhead: The US president claims he can secure a better deal than the 2015 agreement signed by Barack Obama, but Tehran is refusing to grant Washington even a symbolic victory on the issue.

title: The post-May '68 Christian philosophy now being embraced by the American far right
subhead: Trump supporters often blame the cultural upheavals triggered by the May 1968 protests in France for sowing the seeds of 'wokeness.' Yet some of them are now claiming a different French school of thought, which took shape at Stanford University under the influence of René Girard.
